title: Contributing to mapSpain
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("rOpenSpain/mapSpain", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Get started
text: Tip examples vignettes, see full site https://ropenspain.github.io/mapSpain/. mapSpain provides geographical information Spain several levels. mapSpain provides sf objects Autonomous Communities Cities, provinces, municipalities NUTS levels Spain. also provides hexbin maps complementary geometries, demarcation lines around Canary Islands. mapSpain provides access static map tiles Spain’s public institutions. Tiles can represented static maps mapSpain::esp_get_tiles() R leaflet map using mapSpain::addProviderEspTiles(). mapSpain also includes dictionary translates province region names English, Spanish, Catalan, Basque Galician, converts coding standards NUTS, ISO2 INE coding system.

title: Get started
text: mapSpain provides dataset tile caching. Set cache directory : mapSpain relies giscoR downloading files, packages can share cache settings. Using cache directory packages speeds data loading session.
code: esp_set_cache_dir("./path/to/location")

title: Get started
text: examples show key mapSpain workflows: Example: map Spain Example: provinces Andalusia Example: municipalities Basque Country
code: library(mapSpain) library(ggplot2)  country <- esp_get_spain() lines <- esp_get_can_box()  ggplot(country) +   geom_sf(fill = "cornsilk", color = "#887e6a") +   labs(title = "Map of Spain") +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fffff3"),     panel.border = element_rect(       colour = "#887e6a",       fill = NA,     ),     text = element_text(       family = "serif",       face = "bold"     )   ) # Plot provinces.  andalucia <- esp_get_prov_siane("Andalucia")  ggplot(andalucia) +   geom_sf(fill = "darkgreen", color = "white") +   theme_bw() # Plot municipalities.  euskadi_ccaa <- esp_get_ccaa_siane("Euskadi") euskadi <- esp_get_munic_siane(region = "Euskadi")  # Use the dictionary.  euskadi$name_eu <- esp_dict_translate(euskadi$ine.prov.name, lang = "eu")  ggplot(euskadi_ccaa) +   geom_sf(fill = "grey50") +   geom_sf(data = euskadi, aes(fill = name_eu)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("red2", "darkgreen", "ivory2")) +   labs(     fill = "",     title = "Euskal Autonomia Erkidegoko",     subtitle = "Probintziak"   ) +   theme_void() +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     plot.subtitle = element_text(face = "italic")   )

title: Get started
text: Analyze distribution women Autonomous Community City ggplot2: Percentage women Autonomous Community City (2025)
code: library(dplyr)  census <- mapSpain::pobmun25 |>   select(-name)  # Extract Autonomous Community or City codes from the base dataset. codelist <- mapSpain::esp_codelist |>   select(cpro, codauto) |>   distinct()  census_ccaa <- census |>   left_join(codelist) |>   # Summarize by Autonomous Community or City.   group_by(codauto) |>   summarise(pob25 = sum(pob25), men = sum(men), women = sum(women)) |>   mutate(     porc_women = women / pob25,     porc_women_lab = paste0(round(100 * porc_women, 2), "%")   )  # Merge into spatial data. ccaa_sf <- esp_get_ccaa() |>   left_join(census_ccaa)  can <- esp_get_can_box()  # Plot with ggplot. library(ggplot2)  ggplot(ccaa_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = porc_women), color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_sf(data = can, color = "grey70") +   geom_sf_label(     aes(label = porc_women_lab),     fill = "white",     alpha = 0.5,     size = 3,     linewidth = 0   ) +   scale_fill_gradientn(     colors = hcl.colors(10, "Blues", rev = TRUE),     n.breaks = 10,     labels = scales::label_percent(),     guide = guide_legend(title = "% women", position = "inside")   ) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.1, 0.6)) +   labs(caption = "Source: CartoBase ANE 2006-2024 CC-BY 4.0 ign.es, INE")

title: Get started
text: example demonstrates mapSpain can used create thematic maps. plotting, use ggplot2 package, though package handles sf objects, tmap, mapsf leaflet, also used. Population density Spain (2025)
code: # Calculate population density in Spain. library(sf)  pop <- mapSpain::pobmun25 |>   select(-name)  munic <- esp_get_munic_siane(rawcols = TRUE) |>   # Use the area field available in the SIANE data.   mutate(area_km2 = st_area_sh * 10000)  munic_pop <- munic |>   left_join(pop) |>   mutate(dens = pob25 / area_km2)  br <- c(-Inf, 10, 25, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 5000, 10000, Inf)  munic_pop$cuts <- cut(munic_pop$dens, br)  ggplot(munic_pop) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = cuts), color = NA, linewidth = 0) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("grey5", hcl.colors(length(br) - 2, "Spectral")),     labels = prettyNum(c(0, br[-1]), big.mark = ","),     guide = guide_legend(       title = "Pop. per km2",       direction = "horizontal",       nrow = 1     )   ) +   labs(title = "Population density in Spain (2025)") +   theme_void() +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),     plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black"),     text = element_text(colour = "white"),     legend.position = "bottom",     legend.title.position = "top",     legend.text.position = "bottom",     legend.key.width = unit(30, "pt")   ) +   labs(caption = "Source: CartoBase ANE 2006-2024 CC-BY 4.0 ign.es, INE")

title: Get started
text: need plot Spain alongside countries, use giscoR package, installed dependency mapSpain. basic example: mapSpain giscoR example
code: library(giscoR)  # Set the same resolution for a perfect fit. res <- 3  all_countries <- gisco_get_countries(resolution = res) |>   st_transform(3035)  eu_countries <- gisco_get_countries(   resolution = res,   region = "EU" ) |>   st_transform(3035)  ccaa <- esp_get_ccaa(   moveCAN = FALSE,   resolution = res ) |>   st_transform(3035)  # Plot. ggplot(all_countries) +   geom_sf(fill = "#DFDFDF", color = "#656565") +   geom_sf(data = eu_countries, fill = "#FDFBEA", color = "#656565") +   geom_sf(data = ccaa, fill = "#C12838", color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1) +   # Center in Europe: EPSG 3035   coord_sf(     xlim = c(2377294, 7453440),     ylim = c(1313597, 5628510)   ) +   theme(     panel.background = element_blank(),     panel.grid = element_line(       colour = "#DFDFDF",       linetype = "dotted"     )   ) +   labs(caption = giscoR::gisco_attributions("es"))

title: Get started
text: mapSpain provides interface working static map tiles. can download tiles .png .jpeg, depending tile service, use alongside sf objects. mapSpain also includes plugin leaflet R package, allows add several basemaps interactive maps. services implemented leaflet-providersESP Leaflet plugin. available providers listed . Note working static map tiles, set moveCAN = FALSE esp_get_* functions. See Displacing Canary Islands esp_move_can().

title: mapSpain: maps of Spain in R
text: mapSpain helps create maps main administrative levels Spain. also supports static map tiles WMS WMTS services, either georeferenced rasters static maps layers interactive leaflet maps. package also includes helpers translate convert Spanish subdivision names codes. helpers make easier join, clean transform data, whether data spatial. main data sources used mapSpain : GISCO (Eurostat), giscoR package. Instituto Geográfico Nacional (IGN). Spanish public institutions publish WMTS WMS tile services (https://www.idee.es/web/idee/segun-tipo-de-servicio). functions return sf objects SpatRaster objects terra package. Package website: https://ropenspain.github.io/mapSpain/. Use r-universe: Figure 1: Municipalities Galicia can also inspect dataset interactively: next example compares mapSpain packages provide sf SpatVector objects country boundaries. Figure 2: Comparison boundary resolution across map sources. boundaries shown orthophoto Ferrol estuary. rnaturalearth: lower boundary precision. mapSpain: good boundary precision use case. GADM (geodata): high boundary precision. geoBoundaries (geobounds): good boundary precision use case. mapSpain uses web resources. default, downloaded files stored tempdir() reuse current session. Use esp_set_cache_dir() choose user-specific download directory. Add install = TRUE make cache configuration persistent across sessions.
code: install.packages("mapSpain", dependencies = TRUE) # Enable this universe. install.packages(   "mapSpain",   repos = c(     "https://ropenspain.r-universe.dev",     "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ),   dependencies = TRUE ) install.packages("pak") pak::pak("rOpenSpain/mapSpain", dependencies = TRUE) library(mapSpain) library(tidyverse)  galicia <- esp_get_munic_siane(region = "Galicia") |>   # Standardize labels.   mutate(Provincia = esp_dict_translate(ine.prov.name, "es"))  ggplot(galicia) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = Provincia), color = "grey70") +   labs(title = "Provinces of Galicia") +   scale_fill_discrete(type = hcl.colors(4, "Blues")) +   theme_bw() library(reactable)  reactable(   galicia,   searchable = TRUE,   striped = TRUE,   filterable = TRUE,   height = 350,   elementId = "munis-galicia" ) library(sf) # Spatial data manipulation.  # rnaturalearth library(rnaturalearth) esp_rnat <- ne_countries("large", country = "Spain", returnclass = "sf") |>   st_transform(3857)  # mapSpain. esp_mapspain <- esp_get_spain(epsg = 4326) |>   st_transform(3857)  # geodata (GADM) library(geodata) esp_geodata <- geodata::gadm("ES",   path = ".", level = 0 ) |>   # Convert from SpatVector to an sf object.   sf::st_as_sf() |>   st_transform(3857)  # geobounds library(geobounds) esp_geobounds <- geobounds::gb_get_adm0("ESP",   cache_dir = "." ) |>   st_transform(3857)  # Orthophoto of the Ferrol estuary. tile <- esp_get_munic_siane(munic = "Ferrol", epsg = 3857) |>   esp_get_tiles("PNOA", bbox_expand = 0.5, zoommin = 1)  # Prepare the plot. library(tidyterra)  esp_all <- bind_rows(esp_rnat, esp_mapspain, esp_geodata, esp_geobounds)  esp_all$source <- as_factor(c(   "rnaturalearth",   "mapSpain",   "geodata (GADM)",   "geoBoundaries" ))  ggplot(esp_all) +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = tile, maxcell = Inf, alpha = 0.5) +   geom_sf(     aes(color = source, linetype = source),     fill = NA,     show.legend = "line",     linewidth = 0.5,     alpha = 0.7   ) +   coord_sf(     crs = 4326,     xlim = c(-8.384421, -8.154413),     ylim = c(43.43201, 43.59545),     expand = FALSE   ) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "black", "blue", "orange")) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "dotted", "dashed", "twodash")) +   theme_void() +   labs(title = "Ferrol estuary") esp_set_cache_dir("~/R/mapslib/mapSpain", install = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)  #> ℹ mapSpain cache directory is C:/Users/XXXX/Documents/R/mapslib/mapSpain.  munic <- esp_get_munic_siane(verbose = TRUE)  #> ℹ Cache directory is C:/Users/XXXX/Documents/R/mapslib/mapSpain/siane. #> ✔ File already cached: C:/Users/XXXX/Documents/R/mapslib/mapSpain/siane/se89_3_admin_muni_a_x.gpkg. #> ℹ Cache directory is C:/Users/diego/Documents/R/mapslib/GISCO/siane. #> ✔ File already cached: C:/Users/XXXX/Documents/R/mapslib/mapSpain/siane/se89_3_admin_muni_a_y.gpkg

title: mapSpain: maps of Spain in R
text: mapSpain helps create maps main administrative levels Spain. also supports static map tiles WMS WMTS services, either georeferenced rasters static maps layers interactive leaflet maps. package also includes helpers translate convert Spanish subdivision names codes. helpers make easier join, clean transform data, whether data spatial. main data sources used mapSpain : GISCO (Eurostat), giscoR package. Instituto Geográfico Nacional (IGN). Spanish public institutions publish WMTS WMS tile services (https://www.idee.es/web/idee/segun-tipo-de-servicio). functions return sf objects SpatRaster objects terra package. Package website: https://ropenspain.github.io/mapSpain/.

title: mapSpain: maps of Spain in R
text: mapSpain provides two related functions working names codes: esp_dict_region_code() converts text labels Autonomous Community City province codes. Supported coding standards ISO2, NUTS INE codes (codauto cpro). esp_dict_translate() translates text Spanish, English, Catalan, Galician Basque. functions also useful outside spatial workflows, example need standardize Autonomous Community City province codes ISCIII COVID data.
code: vals <- c("Errioxa", "Coruna", "Gerona", "Madrid")  esp_dict_region_code(vals, destination = "nuts") #> [1] "ES23"  "ES111" "ES512" "ES30" esp_dict_region_code(vals, destination = "cpro") #> [1] "26" "15" "17" "28" esp_dict_region_code(vals, destination = "iso2") #> [1] "ES-RI" "ES-C"  "ES-GI" "ES-MD"  # Convert from ISO2 to other codes.  iso2vals <- c("ES-M", "ES-S", "ES-SG") esp_dict_region_code(iso2vals, origin = "iso2") #> [1] "Madrid"    "Cantabria" "Segovia"  iso2vals <- c("ES-GA", "ES-CT", "ES-PV")  esp_dict_region_code(iso2vals, origin = "iso2", destination = "nuts") #> [1] "ES11" "ES51" "ES21"  # Support mixed levels. valsmix <- c("Centro", "Andalucia", "Seville", "Menorca") esp_dict_region_code(valsmix, destination = "nuts") #> [1] "ES4"   "ES61"  "ES618" "ES533"  esp_dict_region_code(c("Murcia", "Las Palmas", "Aragón"), destination = "iso2") #> [1] "ES-MC" "ES-GC" "ES-AR" vals <- c("La Rioja", "Sevilla", "Madrid", "Jaen", "Orense", "Baleares")  esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "en") #> [1] "La Rioja"         "Seville"          "Madrid"           "Jaén"             #> [5] "Ourense"          "Balearic Islands" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "es") #> [1] "La Rioja" "Sevilla"  "Madrid"   "Jaén"     "Orense"   "Baleares" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "ca") #> [1] "La Rioja"      "Sevilla"       "Madrid"        "Jaén"          #> [5] "Ourense"       "Illes Balears"  esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "eu") #> [1] "Errioxa"         "Sevilla"         "Madril"          "Jaén"            #> [5] "Ourense"         "Balear Uharteak" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "ga") #> [1] "A Rioxa"        "Sevilla"        "Madrid"         "Xaén"           #> [5] "Ourense"        "Illas Baleares"

title: mapSpain: maps of Spain in R
text: mapSpain includes functions retrieve political boundaries several levels: Whole country. NUTS (Eurostat). Eurostat statistical classification, levels 0 (country), 1, 2 (Autonomous Communities) 3. Autonomous Communities Cities. Provinces. Municipalities. Autonomous Communities Cities, provinces municipalities, two families functions: esp_get_xxxx() GISCO data esp_get_xxxx_siane() IGN data. information available different projections resolution levels. Figure 3: Map Spain default, mapSpain functions move Canary Islands closer mainland improve visualization. Disable behavior moveCAN = FALSE need geometries original position. package also provides helpers drawing boxes around inset map. See examples reference page. Figure 4: Map Spain displaced Canary Islands Use moveCAN = FALSE working static map tiles, interactive maps spatial analysis. Figure 5: NUTS 1 regions Spain Figure 6: NUTS 3 regions Spain Figure 7: Autonomous Communities Cities Spain Passing higher-level entity, Andalusia, returns provinces within entity. Figure 8: Extracting provinces Autonomous Communities Cities Figure 9: Extracting municipalities Grid maps available squares hexagons provinces Autonomous Communities. () Squares (b) Hexagons Figure 10: Grid maps mapSpain
code: esp <- esp_get_spain_siane(moveCAN = FALSE)  ggplot(esp) +   geom_sf(fill = "#f9cd94") +   theme_light() esp_can <- esp_get_spain() can_prov <- esp_get_can_provinces() can_box <- esp_get_can_box()  ggplot(esp_can) +   geom_sf() +   geom_sf(data = can_prov) +   geom_sf(data = can_box) nuts1 <- esp_get_nuts(resolution = 60, epsg = 3035, nuts_level = 1)  ggplot(nuts1) +   geom_sf() +   labs(title = "NUTS 1: low resolution") # Balearic Islands NUTS 3. nuts3_baleares <- c("ES531", "ES532", "ES533") paste(esp_dict_region_code(nuts3_baleares, "nuts"), collapse = ", ") #> [1] "Eivissa y Formentera, Mallorca, Menorca"  nuts3_sf <- esp_get_nuts(region = nuts3_baleares, resolution = 1)  ggplot(nuts3_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = NAME_LATN)) +   labs(fill = "Balearic Islands: NUTS 3") +   scale_fill_viridis_d() +   theme_minimal() ccaa <- esp_get_ccaa(   ccaa = c(     "Catalunya",     "Comunidad Valenciana",     "Aragón",     "Baleares"   ),   resolution = 3 )  ccaa <- ccaa |>   mutate(ccaa_cat = esp_dict_translate(ine.ccaa.name, "ca"))  ggplot(ccaa) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = ccaa_cat)) +   labs(fill = "Autonomous Communities and Cities") +   theme_minimal() +   scale_fill_discrete(type = hcl.colors(4, "Plasma")) provs <- esp_get_prov_siane(c(   "Andalucía",   "Ciudad Real",   "Murcia",   "Ceuta",   "Melilla" ))  ggplot(provs) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = prov.shortname.es), alpha = 0.9) +   scale_fill_discrete(type = hcl.colors(12, "Cividis")) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(fill = "Provinces") munic <- esp_get_munic_siane(region = "Segovia") |>   # Example data: INE population.   left_join(     mapSpain::pobmun25 |>       select(-name),     by = c("cpro", "cmun")   )  ggplot(munic) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = pob25), alpha = 0.9, color = NA) +   scale_fill_gradientn(     colors = hcl.colors(100, "Inferno"),     n.breaks = 10,     labels = scales::label_comma(),     guide = guide_legend()   ) +   labs(     fill = "Population",     title = "Population in Segovia",     subtitle = "INE data (2025)"   ) +   theme_void() +   theme(     plot.background = element_rect("grey80"),     text = element_text(face = "bold"),     plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),     plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)   ) cuad <- esp_get_hex_ccaa() hex <- esp_get_grid_prov()  ggplot(cuad) +   geom_sf() +   geom_sf_text(aes(label = iso2.ccaa.code)) +   theme_void()  ggplot(hex) +   geom_sf() +   geom_sf_text(aes(label = iso2.prov.code)) +   theme_void()

title: mapSpain: maps of Spain in R
text: mapSpain can also use static map tiles, satellite imagery, basemaps roads, provided different public institutions (https://www.idee.es/web/idee/segun-tipo-de-servicio). tiles can used create static maps, 3- 4-band raster layers, backgrounds interactive maps leaflet package. providers taken leaflet leaflet-providersESP plugin. Several options available composing maps static map tiles: Figure 11: Static map tile extraction Figure 12: Static map tiles mask Static map tiles can used backgrounds static interactive maps.
code: madrid_munis <- esp_get_munic_siane(region = "Madrid", epsg = 3857) base_pnoa <- esp_get_tiles(madrid_munis, "PNOA", bbox_expand = 0.1, zoommin = 1) library(tidyterra) ggplot() + geom_spatraster_rgb(data = base_pnoa) + geom_sf( data = madrid_munis, color = "blue", fill = "blue", alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 0.5 ) + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Municipalities in Madrid") # Use the `mask` option. madrid <- esp_get_munic_siane(munic = "^Madrid$", epsg = 3857) madrid_mask <- esp_get_tiles( madrid, "IDErioja.Claro", mask = TRUE, crop = TRUE, zoommin = 2 ) ggplot() + geom_spatraster_rgb(data = madrid_mask) + theme_void() + labs( title = "Static map tiles of Madrid", caption = "CC BY 4.0 www.iderioja.org" ) stations <- esp_get_railway(spatialtype = "point", epsg = 4326) library(leaflet) # Create an icon. iconurl <- "https://ropenspain.github.io/mapSpain/icons/train.png" train_icon <- makeIcon(iconurl, iconurl, 18, 18) leaflet(stations, elementId = "railway", width = "100%", height = "60vh") |> addProviderEspTiles("IDErioja.Claro", group = "Base") |> addProviderEspTiles("MTN", group = "MTN") |> addProviderEspTiles("RedTransporte.Carreteras", group = "Roads") |> addProviderEspTiles( "RedTransporte.Ferroviario", group = "Railway lines" ) |> addMarkers( icon = train_icon, group = "Stations", popup = sprintf( "%s", stations$rotulo ) |> lapply(htmltools::HTML) ) |> addLayersControl( baseGroups = c("Base", "MTN"), overlayGroups = c("Stations", "Railway lines", "Roads"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) |> hideGroup(c("Railway lines", "Roads"))

code: madrid_munis <- esp_get_munic_siane(region = "Madrid", epsg = 3857) base_pnoa <- esp_get_tiles(madrid_munis, "PNOA", bbox_expand = 0.1, zoommin = 1)  library(tidyterra)  ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = base_pnoa) +   geom_sf(     data = madrid_munis,     color = "blue",     fill = "blue",     alpha = 0.25,     linewidth = 0.5   ) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Municipalities in Madrid")  # Use the `mask` option. madrid <- esp_get_munic_siane(munic = "^Madrid$", epsg = 3857)  madrid_mask <- esp_get_tiles(   madrid,   "IDErioja.Claro",   mask = TRUE,   crop = TRUE,   zoommin = 2 )  ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = madrid_mask) +   theme_void() +   labs(     title = "Static map tiles of Madrid",     caption = "CC BY 4.0 www.iderioja.org"   )

title: mapSpain: maps of Spain in R
text: Static map tiles can used backgrounds static interactive maps.
code: stations <- esp_get_railway(spatialtype = "point", epsg = 4326) library(leaflet) # Create an icon. iconurl <- "https://ropenspain.github.io/mapSpain/icons/train.png" train_icon <- makeIcon(iconurl, iconurl, 18, 18) leaflet(stations, elementId = "railway", width = "100%", height = "60vh") |> addProviderEspTiles("IDErioja.Claro", group = "Base") |> addProviderEspTiles("MTN", group = "MTN") |> addProviderEspTiles("RedTransporte.Carreteras", group = "Roads") |> addProviderEspTiles( "RedTransporte.Ferroviario", group = "Railway lines" ) |> addMarkers( icon = train_icon, group = "Stations", popup = sprintf( "%s", stations$rotulo ) |> lapply(htmltools::HTML) ) |> addLayersControl( baseGroups = c("Base", "MTN"), overlayGroups = c("Stations", "Railway lines", "Roads"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) |> hideGroup(c("Railway lines", "Roads"))

title: Working with static map tiles
text: example shows combine multiple tiles static map. focus layers provided La Rioja’s Infraestructura de Datos Espaciales (IDERioja). Warning working static map tiles, set moveCAN = FALSE, otherwise imagery Canary Islands may inaccurate. Figure 1: Municipal boundaries Logroño using tile basemap tiles can loaded without alpha (transparency) value, controls layer transparency: Figure 2: Map Way St. James Galicia following example repeats code transparent = TRUE: Figure 3: Example using alpha values combine different map tile layers. Now two tiles overlap desired transparency. Another useful feature tile masking, supports advanced maps: Figure 4: Example combining tile types masking sf object.
code: library(mapSpain) library(sf) library(ggplot2) library(tidyterra)  # Logroño lgn_borders <- esp_get_munic_siane(munic = "Logroño")  # Convert to Mercator (EPSG:3857), a common choice when working with tiles. lgn_borders <- st_transform(lgn_borders, 3857)  tile_lgn <- esp_get_tiles(lgn_borders, "IDErioja", bbox_expand = 0.5)  ggplot(lgn_borders) +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = tile_lgn) +   geom_sf(fill = NA, linewidth = 2, color = "blue") galicia <- esp_get_ccaa_siane("Galicia", epsg = 3857)  # Example without transparency. basemap <- esp_get_tiles(   galicia,   "IDErioja.Claro",   zoommin = 1,   crop = TRUE,   bbox_expand = 0 ) tile_opaque <- esp_get_tiles(   galicia,   "CaminoDeSantiago",   verbose = TRUE,   transparent = FALSE,   crop = TRUE,   bbox_expand = 0 )  ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = basemap) +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = tile_opaque) +   theme_void() # Example with transparency. tile_alpha <- esp_get_tiles(   galicia,   "CaminoDeSantiago",   transparent = TRUE,   crop = TRUE,   bbox_expand = 0 )  # Use the same plotting code as above. ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = basemap) +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = tile_alpha) +   theme_void() rioja <- esp_get_prov_siane("La Rioja", epsg = 3857)  basemap <- esp_get_tiles(rioja, "PNOA", bbox_expand = 0.1, zoommin = 1)  masked <- esp_get_tiles(rioja, "IDErioja", mask = TRUE, zoommin = 1)  ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = basemap, maxcell = 10e6) +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = masked, maxcell = 10e6)

title: Working with static map tiles
text: can use esp_get_tiles() get static map tiles providers, example OpenStreetMap. Figure 5: Example basemap using OpenStreetMap example uses ThunderForest, provider needs API key: Figure 6: Example basemap using ThunderForest
code: osm_spec <- list(   id = "OSM",   q = "https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png" )  madrid_city <- esp_get_munic_siane(munic = "^Madrid$", epsg = 3857) madrid_osm <- esp_get_tiles(madrid_city, type = osm_spec, zoommin = 1)  ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = madrid_osm) +   geom_sf(data = madrid_city, fill = NA) # Skip if no API key is available. apikey <- Sys.getenv("THUNDERFOREST_API_KEY", "") if (nzchar(apikey)) {   thunder_spec <- list(     id = "ThunderForest",     q = paste0(       "https://tile.thunderforest.com/transport/{z}/{x}/{y}.png?apikey=",       apikey     )   )   madrid_thunder <- esp_get_tiles(madrid_city, type = thunder_spec, zoommin = 1)    ggplot() +     geom_spatraster_rgb(data = madrid_thunder) +     geom_sf(data = madrid_city, fill = NA) }

title: Working with static map tiles
text: mapSpain provides plugin leaflet package. quick examples: map showing population density Spain 2025:
code:
library(leaflet) tenerife_leaf <- esp_get_nuts( region = "Tenerife", resolution = 3, epsg = 4326, moveCAN = FALSE ) bbox <- as.double(round(st_bbox(tenerife_leaf) + c(-1, -1, 1, 1), 2)) # Start the leaflet map. m <- leaflet( tenerife_leaf, elementId = "tenerife-earthquakes", width = "100%", height = "60vh", options = leafletOptions(minZoom = 9, maxZoom = 18) ) # Add layers. m <- m |> addProviderEspTiles("IDErioja.Relieve") |> addPolygons(color = NA, fillColor = "red", group = "Polygon") |> addProviderEspTiles("Geofisica.Terremotos365dias", group = "Earthquakes") # Add layer controls and bounds. m |> addLayersControl( overlayGroups = c("Polygon", "Earthquakes"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) |> setMaxBounds(bbox[1], bbox[2], bbox[3], bbox[4]) library(leaflet) library(dplyr) munic <- esp_get_munic_siane( year = "2025-01-01", epsg = 4326, moveCAN = FALSE, rawcols = TRUE ) |> # Use the area field available in the SIANE data. mutate(area_km2 = st_area_sh * 10000) # Get population data. pop <- mapSpain::pobmun25 |> select(-name) # Join population data. munic_pop <- munic |> left_join(pop) |> mutate( dens = pob25 / area_km2, dens_label = prettyNum(round(dens, 2), big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",") ) # Create leaflet map. bins <- c(0, 10, 25, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 5000, 10000, Inf) pal <- colorBin("inferno", domain = munic_pop$dens, bins = bins, reverse = TRUE) labels <- sprintf( "%s
%s
%s pers. / km2", munic_pop$rotulo, munic_pop$ine.prov.name, munic_pop$dens_label ) |> lapply(htmltools::HTML) leaflet(elementId = "SpainDemo", width = "100%", height = "60vh") |> setView(lng = -3.684444, lat = 40.308611, zoom = 5) |> addProviderEspTiles("IDErioja") |> addPolygons( data = munic_pop, fillColor = ~ pal(dens), fillOpacity = 0.6, color = "#44444", weight = 0.5, smoothFactor = 0.1, opacity = 1, highlightOptions = highlightOptions( color = "white", weight = 1, bringToFront = TRUE ), popup = labels ) |> addLegend( pal = pal, values = bins, opacity = 0.7, title = paste0( "Pop. Density km2
", "(2025)" ), position = "bottomright" )

title:
mapSpain | Administrative boundaries of Spain, including Autonomous
Communities and Cities, provinces, municipalities and NUTS
text: following examples highlight key features mapSpain.  can combine sf objects static map tiles.
code: library(mapSpain) library(sf) library(dplyr) census <- mapSpain::pobmun25 |>   select(-name)  # Extract Autonomous Community or City codes from the base dataset. codelist <- mapSpain::esp_codelist |>   select(cpro, codauto) |>   distinct()  census_ccaa <- census |>   left_join(codelist) |>   # Summarize by Autonomous Community or City.   group_by(codauto) |>   summarise(pob25 = sum(pob25), men = sum(men), women = sum(women)) |>   mutate(     porc_women = women / pob25,     porc_women_lab = paste0(round(100 * porc_women, 2), "%")   )  # Merge into spatial data. ccaa_sf <- esp_get_ccaa() |>   left_join(census_ccaa)  can <- esp_get_can_box()  # Plot with ggplot. library(ggplot2)  ggplot(ccaa_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = porc_women), color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_sf(data = can, color = "grey70") +   geom_sf_label(     aes(label = porc_women_lab),     fill = "white",     alpha = 0.5,     size = 3,     linewidth = 0   ) +   scale_fill_gradientn(     colors = hcl.colors(10, "Blues", rev = TRUE),     n.breaks = 10,     labels = scales::label_percent(),     guide = guide_legend(title = "% women", position = "inside")   ) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.1, 0.6)) +   labs(caption = "Source: CartoBase ANE 2006-2024 CC-BY 4.0 ign.es, INE") # Get census data. census <- mapSpain::pobmun25 |>   mutate(porc_women = women / pob25) |>   select(cpro, cmun, porc_women)  # Get municipality and province geometries. shape <- esp_get_munic_siane(region = "Segovia", epsg = 3857) provs <- esp_get_prov_siane(epsg = 3857)  shape_pop <- shape |> left_join(census)  tile <- esp_get_tiles(shape_pop, type = "IDErioja.Relieve", zoommin = 1)  # Plot.  library(ggplot2) library(tidyterra)  lims <- as.vector(terra::ext(tile))  ggplot(remove_missing(shape_pop, na.rm = TRUE)) +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = tile, maxcell = 10e6) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = porc_women), color = NA) +   geom_sf(data = provs, fill = NA) +   scale_fill_gradientn(     colours = hcl.colors(10, "RdYlBu", alpha = 0.5),     n.breaks = 8,     labels = function(x) {       sprintf("%1.0f%%", 100 * x)     },     guide = guide_legend(title = "")   ) +   coord_sf(     xlim = lims[c(1, 2)],     ylim = lims[c(3, 4)],     expand = FALSE   ) +   labs(     title = "% women in Segovia by town (2025)",     caption = paste0(       "Source: INE, CC BY 4.0 www.iderioja.org, ",       "CartoBase ANE 2006-2024 CC-BY 4.0 ign.es"     )   ) +   theme_void() +   theme(     title = element_text(face = "bold")   )

text: need plot Spain alongside countries, use giscoR package, installed dependency mapSpain. basic example:
code: library(giscoR)  # Set the same resolution for a perfect fit.  res <- "20"  all_countries <- gisco_get_countries(resolution = res) |>   st_transform(3035)  eu_countries <- gisco_get_countries(resolution = res, region = "EU") |>   st_transform(3035)  ccaa <- esp_get_ccaa(moveCAN = FALSE, resolution = res) |>   st_transform(3035)  library(ggplot2)  ggplot(all_countries) +   geom_sf(fill = "#DFDFDF", color = "#656565") +   geom_sf(data = eu_countries, fill = "#FDFBEA", color = "#656565") +   geom_sf(data = ccaa, fill = "#C12838", color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1) +   # Center on Europe: EPSG 3035.   coord_sf(xlim = c(2377294, 7453440), ylim = c(1313597, 5628510)) +   theme(     panel.background = element_blank(),     panel.grid = element_line(colour = "#DFDFDF", linetype = "dotted")   ) +   labs(caption = giscoR::gisco_attributions("es"))

title: Convert and translate Spanish subdivision names and codes — esp_dict_region_code
text: sourcevar Character string. Vector contains codes names converted. origin, destination Character string. Coding scheme origin destination. One "text", "nuts", "iso2", "codauto" "cpro". lang Character string. Target language code, available values: "es": Spanish. "en": English. "ca": Catalan. "ga": Galician. "eu": Basque. Logical. TRUE function returns possible translations input named list. FALSE (default) single preferred translation per input returned character vector.

title: Convert and translate Spanish subdivision names and codes — esp_dict_region_code
text: esp_dict_region_code() returns character vector converted subdivision identifiers names. value matched, corresponding element NA warning emitted via cli::cli_alert_warning(). esp_dict_translate() translates vector names one language another. = FALSE, character vector translated name element sourcevar. = TRUE, named list returned element contains available translations corresponding input value.

title: Convert and translate Spanish subdivision names and codes — esp_dict_region_code
text: function uses internal dictionaries together countrycode map schemes. origin == destination == "text" input returned unchanged. Mixing names different administrative levels, example Autonomous Community City province, may produce NA values entries.

title: Convert and translate Spanish subdivision names and codes — esp_dict_region_code
code: vals <- c("Errioxa", "Coruna", "Gerona", "Madrid")  esp_dict_region_code(vals) #> ℹ No conversion. `origin` equal to `destination` ("text") #> [1] "Errioxa" "Coruna"  "Gerona"  "Madrid"  esp_dict_region_code(vals, destination = "nuts") #> [1] "ES23"  "ES111" "ES512" "ES30"  esp_dict_region_code(vals, destination = "cpro") #> [1] "26" "15" "17" "28" esp_dict_region_code(vals, destination = "iso2") #> [1] "ES-RI" "ES-C"  "ES-GI" "ES-MD"  # From ISO2 to other codes  iso2vals <- c("ES-M", "ES-S", "ES-SG") esp_dict_region_code(iso2vals, origin = "iso2") #> [1] "Madrid"    "Cantabria" "Segovia"   esp_dict_region_code(iso2vals,   origin = "iso2",   destination = "nuts" ) #> [1] "ES300" "ES130" "ES416" esp_dict_region_code(iso2vals,   origin = "iso2",   destination = "cpro" ) #> [1] "28" "39" "40"  # Mixing levels valsmix <- c("Centro", "Andalucia", "Seville", "Menorca") esp_dict_region_code(valsmix, destination = "nuts") #> [1] "ES4"   "ES61"  "ES618" "ES533"  esp_dict_region_code(valsmix, destination = "codauto") #> ! No match found for "Centro", "Seville", and "Menorca" with `destination = "codauto"`. #> [1] NA   "01" NA   NA   esp_dict_region_code(valsmix, destination = "iso2") #> ! No match found for "Centro" and "Menorca" with `destination = "iso2"`. #> [1] NA      "ES-AN" "ES-SE" NA       vals <- c("La Rioja", "Sevilla", "Madrid", "Jaen", "Orense", "Baleares")  esp_dict_translate(vals) #> [1] "La Rioja"         "Seville"          "Madrid"           "Jaén"             #> [5] "Ourense"          "Balearic Islands" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "es") #> [1] "La Rioja" "Sevilla"  "Madrid"   "Jaén"     "Orense"   "Baleares" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "ca") #> [1] "La Rioja"      "Sevilla"       "Madrid"        "Jaén"          #> [5] "Ourense"       "Illes Balears" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "eu") #> [1] "Errioxa"         "Sevilla"         "Madril"          "Jaén"            #> [5] "Ourense"         "Balear Uharteak" esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "ga") #> [1] "A Rioxa"        "Sevilla"        "Madrid"         "Xaén"           #> [5] "Ourense"        "Illas Baleares"  esp_dict_translate(vals, lang = "ga", all = TRUE) #> $`La Rioja` #> [1] "A Rioxa" "a rioxa" "A RIOXA" #>  #> $Sevilla #> [1] "Sevilla"              "sevilla"              "SEVILLA"              #> [4] "Provincia de Sevilla" "provincia de sevilla" "PROVINCIA DE SEVILLA" #>  #> $Madrid #> [1] "Madrid"               "madrid"               "MADRID"               #> [4] "Provincia de Madrid"  "Comunidade de Madrid" "provincia de madrid"  #> [7] "comunidade de madrid" "PROVINCIA DE MADRID"  "COMUNIDADE DE MADRID" #>  #> $Jaen #>  [1] "Xaén"              "Xaen"              "xaén"              #>  [4] "xaen"              "XAÉN"              "XAEN"              #>  [7] "Provincia de Xaén" "Provincia de Xaen" "provincia de xaén" #> [10] "provincia de xaen" "PROVINCIA DE XAÉN" "PROVINCIA DE XAEN" #>  #> $Orense #> [1] "Ourense"              "ourense" title: Canary Islands inset box and outline — esp_get_can_box
code: provs <- esp_get_prov() box <- esp_get_can_box() line <- esp_get_can_provinces()  library(ggplot2) ggplot(provs) +   geom_sf() +   geom_sf(data = box, linewidth = 0.15) +   geom_sf(data = line, linewidth = 0.15) +   theme_linedraw()   # \donttest{ # Displacing the Canary Islands by a custom offset displace <- c(15, 0) provs_disp <- esp_get_prov(moveCAN = displace) box_disp <- esp_get_can_box(style = "left", moveCAN = displace) line_disp <- esp_get_can_provinces(moveCAN = displace) ggplot(provs_disp) +   geom_sf() +   geom_sf(data = box_disp, linewidth = 0.15) +   geom_sf(data = line_disp, linewidth = 0.15) +   theme_linedraw()   # Example using the polygon style together with other layers library(giscoR) res <- "20" countries <- gisco_get_countries(   res = res, epsg = "4326",   country = c("France", "Portugal", "Andorra", "Morocco", "Argelia") ) can_box <- esp_get_can_box(   style = "poly", epsg = "4326",   moveCAN = c(12.5, 0) ) ccaa <- esp_get_ccaa(res = res, epsg = "4326", moveCAN = c(12.5, 0)) ggplot(countries) +   geom_sf(fill = "#DFDFDF") +   geom_sf(data = can_box, fill = "#C7E7FB", linewidth = 1) +   geom_sf(data = ccaa, fill = "#FDFBEA") +   coord_sf(xlim = c(-10, 4.3), ylim = c(34.6, 44)) +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#C7E7FB"),     panel.grid = element_blank()   )  # }

code: # \donttest{ # Compare municipality centroids against esp_get_capimun().  # Get the municipality boundary. area <- esp_get_munic_siane(munic = "Valladolid", epsg = 3857)  # Area in km2. print(paste0(round(as.double(sf::st_area(area)) / 1000000, 2), " km2")) #> [1] "353.42 km2"  # Extract the centroid. centroid <- sf::st_centroid(area) #> Warning: st_centroid assumes attributes are constant over geometries centroid$type <- "Centroid"  # Compare with capimun. capimun <- esp_get_capimun(munic = "Valladolid", epsg = 3857) capimun$type <- "Capimun"  # Join both point geometries. points <- dplyr::bind_rows(centroid, capimun)  # Check on a plot. library(ggplot2)  ggplot(points) +   geom_sf(data = area, fill = NA, color = "blue") +   geom_sf(data = points, aes(fill = type), size = 5, shape = 21) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("green", "red")) +   labs(title = "Centroid vs. capimun")  # }

title: Territorial Spanish units for statistics (NUTS) dataset — esp_get_nuts
text: GISCO statistical unit dataset represents NUTS (Nomenclature Territorial Units Statistics) statistical regions using multipart polygon, polyline point topology. NUTS geographical information completed attribute tables set cartographic help lines better visualize multipart polygonal regions. NUTS hierarchical system divided 3 levels: NUTS 1: major socioeconomic regions. NUTS 2: basic regions application regional policies. NUTS 3: small regions specific diagnoses. also NUTS 0 level, usually corresponds national boundaries.

URBN_TYPE: Urban-rural typology: 1: Predominantly urban region. 2: Intermediate region. 3: Predominantly rural region. 0: classification provided. title: Set your mapSpain cache directory — esp_set_cache_dir
text: files can read online source without caching using option cache = FALSE. Otherwise source files downloaded computer. mapSpain implements following caching options: occasional use, rely default tempdir()-based cache (install). Modify cache single session setting esp_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/"). reproducible workflows, install persistent cache esp_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/", install = TRUE), persists across R sessions. caching specific files, use cache_dir argument corresponding function. Sometimes cached files may corrupted. case, try re-downloading data setting update_cache = TRUE corresponding function. experience download problems, try downloading file another method save cache_dir. Use verbose = TRUE debug API query esp_detect_cache_dir() identify cache path.

title: mapSpain (development version)
text: Refactored internal helpers downloading reading geospatial files, SIANE file handling, EPSG validation, subdivision-code filtering, municipal metadata enrichment, empty-result messages Canary Islands displacement. intended simplify maintenance without changing public API. Consolidated repeated Autonomous Community City metadata province municipality metadata across GISCO, SIANE, simplified gridmap getters. simplified internal cache handling, sf output finalization, WMTS tile retrieval, dictionary translation -match messages. changes intended improve maintainability without changing user-facing behavior. Reviewed roxygen2 documentation, generated Rd files prose documentation consistent terminology clearer user-facing messages. work completed AI assistance human review. internal refactor developed AI assistance reviewed focused package checks, including devtools::load_all() followed lintr::lint_package().

title: mapSpain 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-26 Migrated package vignettes Quarto. Minimum httr2 version now 1.2.0 ensure compatibility giscoR. Query timeout can controlled options(mapspain_timeout) using httr2::req_timeout(). default value httr2::req_timeout(..., seconds = 300) (5 minutes).

title: mapSpain 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-17 major release introduces full overhaul codebase test suite. Requests now use httr2, cached files reorganized topic-based subfolders easier management. internal changes, existing caches compatible release must rebuilt. transitioned rappdirs::user_config_dir() tools::R_user_dir() managing persistent cache directory. existing cache directory, receive one-time notification migration. package now requires R >= 4.1, dependency updates improve performance maintainability. functions return tidy objects, either tibbles sf objects tibble data. Several new functions arguments added, functions renamed others deprecated. bundled datasets updated latest versions.

title: mapSpain 1.0.0
text: Minimum required R version now 4.1.0. Removed dependency slippymath (#126). esp_get_grid_EEA() deprecated defunct source file longer available. providerEspTileOptions() removed, use leaflet::providerTileOptions() instead. Removed dataset ?esp_munic.sf. Removed dataset ?leaflet.providersESP.df, superseded mapSpain v0.8.0. Removed dataset ?pobmun19, replaced ?pobmun25.

title: mapSpain 1.0.0
text: esp_get_country() renamed esp_get_spain(), old name still works. esp_get_rivers() deprecates resolution spatialtype arguments. Wetlands support moved new esp_get_wetlands() function. esp_getTiles() renamed esp_get_tiles(), old name still works.

title: mapSpain 1.0.0
text: Added dataset ?esp_nuts_2024, replacing ?esp_nuts.sf. esp_get_attributions() retrieves tile provider attributions. esp_get_countries_siane() retrieves countries available SIANE given date. esp_get_rivers() gains new moveCAN argument. esp_get_spain_siane() analogous esp_get_spain() uses SIANE data. esp_get_stations() replaces esp_get_railway(..., spatialtype = "point"). esp_get_tiles() can used providers need API key. esp_siane_bulk_download() downloads SIANE datasets specified cache_dir single step.

title: mapSpain 0.10.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-15 esp_get_comarca() gains new type argument extract official (IGN), statistical (INE), agrarian livestock comarcas (MAPA). esp_get_prov_siane() fixes bug selecting columns (#115).

title: mapSpain 0.9.2
text: CRAN release: 2024-08-26 Adapted functions SIANE 2024 databases. esp_dict_region_code() esp_dict_translate() improve dictionary support.

title: mapSpain 0.9.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-06-10 Updated actions documentation.

title: mapSpain 0.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-23 Changed modifications Canary Islands objects handled (#101). esp_detect_cache_dir() shows current cache directory. esp_move_can() added helper displace standalone sf objects Canary Islands. esp_move_can() used internally functions. layer_spatraster() removed, deprecated mapSpain 0.6.2.

title: mapSpain 0.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-07-12 Improved download NUTS data giscoR. Upgraded ?esp_tiles_providers https://dieghernan.github.io/leaflet-providersESP/ v1.3.3. New providers include IDErioja.Base, IDErioja.Relieve, IDErioja.Claro IDErioja.Oscuro. esp_getTiles() now supports non-OGC-compliant WMTS providers, Stamen OpenStreetMap. See examples.

title: mapSpain 0.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-12-22 Changed package manages tile providers. Upgraded ?leaflet.providersESP.df https://dieghernan.github.io/leaflet-providersESP/ v1.3.2. ?leaflet.providersESP.df superseded favor ?esp_tiles_providers. esp_getTiles() can use custom URL type argument (#88). esp_make_provider() added help create custom providers.

title: mapSpain 0.6.2
text: CRAN release: 2022-08-13 moveCAN now explicit argument relevant functions. Deprecated layer_spatraster(). Use tidyterra::geom_spatraster_rgb() instead. esp_get_comarca() gets comarcas INE. esp_get_hex_prov() esp_get_hex_ccaa() fix geometries. Added functions get simplified maps INE, esp_get_simpl_prov() esp_get_simpl_ccaa().

title: mapSpain 0.6.1

title: mapSpain 0.6.0

New providers include Catastro.BuildingPart, Catastro.AdministrativeBoundary Catastro.AdministrativeUnit. esp_getTiles() gains new options argument.

title: mapSpain 0.5.0

title: mapSpain 0.4.0
